applied_optimizations.name: Baseline PyTorch implementation
applied_optimizations.description: Working PyTorch implementation matching reference. Score: 60 (correct but not optimized).

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile with einops.rearrange
attempted_but_failed.reason: Correctness failure - torch.compile doesn't properly trace einops.rearrange, causing parameter capture issues
attempted_but_failed.error: Output mismatch with huge differences (10^19 scale)

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton kernel with 5D grid
attempted_but_failed.error: program_id axis must be 0, 1, or 2 but got 3

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton kernel with tl.outer
attempted_but_failed.reason: tl.outer not available in ROCm Triton

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton kernel with nested loops and runtime indexing
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton doesn't support indexing tensors with runtime loop variables

notes: Key insight: 37.4% of time is in elementwise copy kernels from rearrange/einsum creating intermediates
Main optimization opportunity: Fuse the einsum operations to reduce memory traffic
torch.compile doesn't work well with einops.rearrange - need native PyTorch reshape/permute
ROCm Triton limitations: no tl.outer, only 3D grids, no runtime tensor indexing
For Triton optimization, need to use manual outer product (b_vals[:, None] * x_vals[None, :])
Should focus on fusing: (1) intra-chunk states einsum, (2) inter-chunk recurrence einsum
The segsum operation (tril + masked_fill) accounts for ~6% and could be optimized
